conceptClusters.title: Retrieved value provenance inheritance and cascade
exitEvidence: Learner traces winning declarations, defaulting, inheritance, and computed values before introducing custom-property substitution.
Learner preserves reader-origin and layer precedence when reusable values change scope or importance.

conceptClusters.title: Unregistered token streams substitution and fallback
exitEvidence: Learner distinguishes a cascaded custom-property token stream from validity in each consuming property at computed-value time.
Learner predicts missing, guaranteed-invalid, empty, nested fallback, shorthand, case-sensitive name, and scoped override results.
Learner builds per-element dependency graphs and identifies every property invalidated by direct, indirect, or fallback-argument cycles.
Learner repairs the earliest broken dependency without hiding unrelated invalid values behind broad fallback chains.

conceptClusters.title: Registered syntax inheritance initial values and interpolation
exitEvidence: Learner validates @property syntax, inherits, and initial-value descriptors including computational independence and registration-order behavior.
Learner proves typed computed values and interpolation while bounding draft status, support evidence, reduced motion, and the unregistered result.

activityDesigns.cumulativeArtifact: A worked evidence board grows from ordinary cascade and inheritance into unregistered substitution, dependency invalidity, registered contracts, and typed interpolation.
activityDesigns.startingState: Learner can trace ordinary cascade winners and inherited values but has not modeled author-defined properties as separate cascaded values.
activityDesigns.completionState: Learner can explain one complete reusable-value path and predict changed unregistered and registered behavior without treating custom properties as programming variables.
activityDesigns.feedbackContract: Feedback stops at the first incorrect value stage and exposes only the evidence required to retry that stage.
activityDesigns.hintContract: Worked guidance reveals one cascade, substitution, registration, or interpolation decision while reserving the changed result.
activityDesigns.correctionContract: A miss routes to a two-value parallel trace, then returns to the original case after an explained correction.

interactions.title: Recover value provenance
interactions.learnerAction: Read a declaration ledger and identify the cascade winner, inherited or initial result, and exact computed value before any var() substitution.
interactions.artifactChange: Evidence board gains an ordinary property provenance row.

evidence.observable: Row separates winning declaration, defaulting choice, inherited source, and computed result.
evidence.changedCase: Move the declaration to a child, add a user rule, and switch the property inheritance definition.

interactions.feedbackTarget: First confused cascade or defaulting stage before custom properties enter.
interactions.correctionRoute: Rank declarations, then apply property-specific inheritance on a parallel tree.

interactions.title: Predict token substitution
interactions.learnerAction: Predict a custom property's cascaded token stream and the different computed results when that stream is inserted into two typed consuming properties.
interactions.artifactChange: Evidence board gains separate custom-property and consumer-value columns.

evidence.observable: Prediction keeps permissive custom-property syntax distinct from consumer grammar and invalid-at-computed-value behavior.
evidence.changedCase: Replace a color token with a length token and swap one consumer property.

interactions.feedbackTarget: First assumption that declaration acceptance proves every consumer is valid.
interactions.correctionRoute: Preserve the token stream, substitute it literally, then parse each consumer grammar.

interactions.title: Inspect a dependency cycle
interactions.learnerAction: Inspect direct and indirect var() edges on one element and identify which custom properties become invalid before their consumers resolve.
interactions.artifactChange: Evidence board gains a per-element dependency graph and invalid nodes.

evidence.observable: Every cycle edge, invalid node, inherited external input, and unaffected branch is explicit.
evidence.changedCase: Add a fallback edge that closes a cycle and move one dependency to the parent.

interactions.feedbackTarget: First missing dependency edge or incorrectly invalidated noncycle node.
interactions.correctionRoute: Draw each var() reference including fallback arguments, then recompute strongly connected nodes.

interactions.title: Explain registered behavior
interactions.learnerAction: Explain how syntax, inherits, initial-value, computed validation, cascade neutrality, and typed interpolation change the same custom property after valid registration.
interactions.artifactChange: Evidence board gains a registered-versus-unregistered contract comparison.

evidence.observable: Explanation distinguishes registration initial value from var() fallback and does not claim registration changes precedence.
evidence.changedCase: Invalidate the typed value, flip inherits, interrupt a transition, and remove registration support.

interactions.feedbackTarget: First conflation of syntax validation, initial behavior, cascade, fallback, or animation.
interactions.correctionRoute: Evaluate descriptors, cascade, computed validation, substitution, and interpolation in that order.

activityDesigns.cumulativeArtifact: A city-service status interface gains purpose-named reusable values, scoped overrides, dependency tests, registered contracts, and motion-safe typed transitions.
activityDesigns.startingState: A working semantic service board repeats literal colors, spacing, timing, and emphasis values across unrelated components.
activityDesigns.completionState: The board has a documented value system whose cascade, substitution, invalidity, registration, and changed-environment behavior are verified.
activityDesigns.feedbackContract: Guided feedback reports the first failed evidence stage and the smallest related inspection, never replacement code.
activityDesigns.hintContract: Hints progress from requirement and provenance question to one parallel token trace, then one structural cue.
activityDesigns.correctionContract: Each failed check requires a prediction, minimal repair, and changed-case rerun before continuing.

interactions.title: Inventory repeated values
interactions.learnerAction: Inspect repeated literals and classify only genuinely shared meaning, leaving coincidental equal values independent.
interactions.artifactChange: Service board gains a semantic value inventory with ownership boundaries.

evidence.observable: Each proposed property names a shared purpose and every excluded literal has a reason.
evidence.changedCase: Change one coincidental literal and one shared policy value independently.

interactions.feedbackTarget: First value grouped by visual equality instead of shared responsibility.
interactions.correctionRoute: Trace consumers and change authority, then split the first unrelated group.

interactions.title: Predict existing inheritance
interactions.learnerAction: Predict existing inherited, initial, unset, and reverted consumer values before replacing literals with reusable values.
interactions.artifactChange: Inventory gains baseline value-provenance expectations.

evidence.observable: Baseline identifies each consumer's current specified and computed source.
evidence.changedCase: Move one component under a different parent and add a reader-origin declaration.

interactions.feedbackTarget: First baseline result inferred from appearance instead of provenance.
interactions.correctionRoute: Trace ordinary cascade and defaulting before introducing any custom property.

interactions.title: Define purpose-named properties
interactions.learnerAction: Code case-consistent custom property names and token streams at the narrowest owner that serves every intended consumer.
interactions.artifactChange: Service board gains its first scoped reusable-value declarations.

evidence.observable: Computed properties exist on intended owners and do not leak unnecessary policy to unrelated subtrees.
evidence.changedCase: Duplicate a component outside the owner and vary custom-property name case.

interactions.feedbackTarget: First global scope, opaque numbering, or case mismatch without purpose evidence.
interactions.correctionRoute: Name responsibility and consumer set, then relocate only the affected declaration.

interactions.title: Consume token streams
interactions.learnerAction: Replace selected literals with var() references while preserving each consuming property's complete grammar and ordinary cascade position.
interactions.artifactChange: Selected service components consume the new value contracts.

evidence.observable: Every substituted token stream parses in its consumer and retains the intended winning declaration.
evidence.changedCase: Move a use into a shorthand and place a competing declaration in another layer.

interactions.feedbackTarget: First consumer whose post-substitution grammar or precedence changes unexpectedly.
interactions.correctionRoute: Expand the resulting declaration text, validate its grammar, then retrace its cascade.

interactions.title: Predict scoped inheritance
interactions.learnerAction: Predict same-element lookup and descendant inheritance through root, section, component, and state-specific custom-property overrides.
interactions.artifactChange: Service board gains a scope-and-inheritance trace.

evidence.observable: Each consumer uses the computed custom property on itself, including inherited and locally overridden values.
evidence.changedCase: Reparent a component, toggle state, and remove its nearest override.

interactions.feedbackTarget: First assumption that var() reads directly from the declaration site.
interactions.correctionRoute: Compute the custom property on the consuming element before substitution.

interactions.title: Code bounded fallback chains
interactions.learnerAction: Code nested var() fallbacks only for explicitly optional missing or guaranteed-invalid inputs and preserve commas inside the fallback value.
interactions.artifactChange: Optional service accents gain bounded recovery paths.

evidence.observable: Fallback runs only for the specified unavailable custom property and yields a valid complete consumer value.
evidence.changedCase: Remove the primary property, invalidate the nested property, and use a comma-bearing fallback.

interactions.feedbackTarget: First fallback that masks an unrelated consumer-grammar error or parses incorrectly.
interactions.correctionRoute: Identify the exact unavailable reference, then retokenize the complete var() function.

interactions.title: Test invalid consumer values
interactions.learnerAction: Test one valid custom token stream in incompatible color, length, and shorthand consumers and record each computed-value consequence.
interactions.artifactChange: Service test matrix gains invalid-at-computed-value cases.

evidence.observable: Results follow each consuming property's invalid computed-value behavior rather than recovering an earlier cascaded declaration.
evidence.changedCase: Swap consumer property, change inheritance behavior, and add an earlier valid declaration.

interactions.feedbackTarget: First claim that the cascade returns to an earlier declaration after computed invalidity.
interactions.correctionRoute: Freeze the cascade winner, substitute, then apply the consumer's invalid computed-value result.

interactions.title: Distinguish empty and initial
interactions.learnerAction: Inspect an empty custom-property value, omitted property, and initial keyword and explain their different substitution and fallback behavior.
interactions.artifactChange: Service matrix gains empty, missing, and guaranteed-invalid rows.

evidence.observable: Rows distinguish valid empty token stream from the guaranteed-invalid initial value and absence after inheritance.
evidence.changedCase: Add whitespace tokens, remove the parent value, and replace empty with initial.

interactions.feedbackTarget: First conflation of empty serialization, missing property, and guaranteed-invalid value.
interactions.correctionRoute: Record token presence and computed custom-property value before evaluating var().

interactions.title: Compose a dependency chain
interactions.learnerAction: Code a one-direction purpose-named dependency chain for service emphasis and prove every edge has a bounded owner.
interactions.artifactChange: Service board gains a documented acyclic dependency chain.

evidence.observable: Graph is acyclic per element and produces valid tokens at all intended consumers.
evidence.changedCase: Override an intermediate property locally and remove the root input.

interactions.feedbackTarget: First hidden reverse dependency or owner mismatch.
interactions.correctionRoute: Draw every var() edge per consuming element, then remove the earliest unjustified edge.

interactions.title: Mutate dependency cycles
interactions.learnerAction: Introduce direct, indirect, and fallback-argument cycles and test invalid nodes, unaffected branches, and consumer outcomes.
interactions.artifactChange: Dependency suite gains three cycle mutations and expected results.

evidence.observable: All and only cycle members become invalid on the element; inherited external nodes remain correctly classified.
evidence.changedCase: Move one edge to an ancestor and add a reference inside a fallback argument.

interactions.feedbackTarget: First cycle membership or per-element boundary error.
interactions.correctionRoute: Rebuild the graph on the changed element and mark strongly connected nodes only.

interactions.title: Register a typed property
interactions.learnerAction: Code a valid @property rule with a narrow syntax, explicit inherits flag, and computationally independent initial value.
interactions.artifactChange: Service board gains its first registered property contract.

evidence.observable: All required descriptors parse, initial value satisfies syntax and independence, and registration is observable.
evidence.changedCase: Remove each descriptor, use a relative initial value, and replace syntax with universal syntax.

interactions.feedbackTarget: First invalid descriptor, syntax string, or dependent initial value.
interactions.correctionRoute: Validate the rule and each descriptor independently before testing declarations.

interactions.title: Predict typed invalidity
interactions.learnerAction: Predict registered computed results for valid, syntax-invalid, missing, inherited, initial, and var()-referenced declarations.
interactions.artifactChange: Contract board gains a typed computed-value table.

evidence.observable: Invalid typed declarations resolve through registered initial behavior and remain distinct from var() fallback selection.
evidence.changedCase: Flip inherits, change syntax type, add a valid var() dependency, and remove the declaration.

interactions.feedbackTarget: First incorrect registered initial, inheritance, or fallback result.
interactions.correctionRoute: Compute the registered property first, then serialize its computed value into the consumer.

interactions.title: Test cascade neutrality
interactions.learnerAction: Test competing registered-property declarations across layers, specificity, importance, and source order without treating typed validity as cascade priority.
interactions.artifactChange: Contract suite gains precedence mutations before typed validation.

evidence.observable: Ordinary cascade chooses the declaration before registered syntax controls its computed result.
evidence.changedCase: Reorder layers, toggle importance, change selector weight, and make the winner syntax-invalid.

interactions.feedbackTarget: First valid lower-priority declaration incorrectly revived after winner invalidity.
interactions.correctionRoute: Freeze the ordinary cascade winner, then apply the registration contract once.

interactions.title: Bound feature support claims
interactions.learnerAction: Explain why a generic @supports custom-property declaration cannot prove registered syntax acceptance and name direct evidence for the target environment.
interactions.artifactChange: Contract board gains an explicit support-evidence boundary.

evidence.observable: Claim distinguishes permissive parse-time acceptance, computed-time typed validation, and observed registration behavior.
evidence.changedCase: Use a syntax-invalid declared value in @supports and test an environment without @property behavior.

interactions.feedbackTarget: First support conclusion based only on permissive declaration parsing.
interactions.correctionRoute: Name the exact runtime behavior needed and observe that behavior directly.

interactions.title: Code typed interpolation
interactions.learnerAction: Code a registered number or color transition and preserve a usable final state when registration or motion is unavailable.
interactions.artifactChange: Service board gains optional typed status interpolation.

evidence.observable: Intermediate computed samples follow the registered type while final meaning remains available without animation.
evidence.changedCase: Interrupt the transition, remove registration support, and request reduced motion.

interactions.feedbackTarget: First animation dependency that hides state or assumes typed interpolation universally.
interactions.correctionRoute: Verify static endpoints first, then sample typed intermediate values only in supported motion conditions.

interactions.title: Explain the completed system
interactions.learnerAction: Explain property ownership, substitution, dependency, registration, cascade, support, and motion decisions using the completed changed-case evidence.
interactions.artifactChange: Service board gains an evidence-linked maintenance note.

evidence.observable: Every abstraction maps to a shared responsibility and verified behavior rather than fewer literal lines alone.
evidence.changedCase: Add a new service component, change reader preferences, invalidate one input, and disable registration.

interactions.feedbackTarget: First architectural claim without a consumer, owner, or changed-case result.
interactions.correctionRoute: Trace the disputed property from authority through each consumer and revise only supported claims.

activityDesigns.cumulativeArtifact: A seasonal community-events theme is completed from partial contracts while guidance fades across cascade, substitution, cycles, registration, and interpolation.
activityDesigns.startingState: The event listing works with partial purpose names, missing substitution tests, one hidden cycle, and incomplete registration descriptors.
activityDesigns.completionState: Learner completes and defends the theme using changed cases without copying the city-service structure or values.
activityDesigns.feedbackContract: Feedback identifies only the failed evidence dimension after commitment.
activityDesigns.hintContract: Faded hints name a value stage, then a smaller parallel case, and disappear after successful correction.
activityDesigns.correctionContract: Every failure requires a changed parallel completion before returning to the event theme.

interactions.title: Complete the value ledger
interactions.learnerAction: Complete missing cascade, inheritance, and computed-value rows for event cards before changing their reusable-value system.
interactions.artifactChange: Event theme gains a verified ordinary-value baseline.

evidence.observable: Every baseline row identifies the winning declaration and defaulting path.
evidence.changedCase: Move a card, add a reader rule, and remove one author declaration.

interactions.feedbackTarget: First missing baseline provenance decision.
interactions.correctionRoute: Recompute the smallest ordinary-property case before completing the changed row.

interactions.title: Code seasonal value contracts
interactions.learnerAction: Code purpose-named seasonal values and scoped event-category overrides without coupling coincidentally equal colors or spacing.
interactions.artifactChange: Event cards gain independent theme and category contracts.

evidence.observable: Each owner and consumer remains correct after independent policy changes.
evidence.changedCase: Change one category, duplicate a card outside the theme, and vary property-name case.

interactions.feedbackTarget: First property with unclear shared responsibility or excess scope.
interactions.correctionRoute: Name the change authority and relocate only the disputed value.

interactions.title: Test theme substitutions
interactions.learnerAction: Test missing, empty, initial, invalid-consumer, nested-fallback, and shorthand substitutions against the completed theme.
interactions.artifactChange: Event theme gains a substitution mutation suite.

evidence.observable: Expected results distinguish unavailable variables from invalid consuming values.
evidence.changedCase: Remove a token, empty another, reset one to initial, and change a consumer grammar.

interactions.feedbackTarget: First fallback activated for the wrong invalidity class.
interactions.correctionRoute: Compute the referenced property, substitute, then validate the complete consumer.

interactions.title: Complete the dependency repair
interactions.learnerAction: Complete a partial graph, locate the hidden fallback-argument cycle, and repair it without flattening useful one-way dependencies.
interactions.artifactChange: Event theme gains an acyclic dependency graph and regression case.

evidence.observable: Repair removes the cycle while preserving intended inherited inputs and unaffected branches.
evidence.changedCase: Move one edge to an ancestor and add a local override that changes graph membership.

interactions.feedbackTarget: First omitted edge or over-broad cycle repair.
interactions.correctionRoute: Redraw all references on the affected element and cut only the earliest unjustified edge.

interactions.title: Finish the registration contract
interactions.learnerAction: Finish a partial @property rule and prove syntax, inherits, independent initial value, typed invalidity, and cascade-neutral behavior.
interactions.artifactChange: Event theme gains a verified registered progress value.

evidence.observable: Registration is valid and every declaration follows cascade before typed computed validation.
evidence.changedCase: Remove a descriptor, use em initial value, invert layer order, and submit an invalid type.

interactions.feedbackTarget: First registration validity or precedence mistake.
interactions.correctionRoute: Validate descriptors, freeze the cascade winner, then compute the typed value.

interactions.title: Explain typed theme motion
interactions.learnerAction: Explain sampled interpolation, interruption, static endpoints, reduced motion, and unsupported registration behavior for the seasonal indicator.
interactions.artifactChange: Event theme gains a bounded motion and support note.

evidence.observable: Explanation proves meaning at endpoints and bounds intermediate claims to observed support.
evidence.changedCase: Interrupt motion, request reduced motion, remove registration, and change value type.

interactions.feedbackTarget: First motion or support claim beyond observed evidence.
interactions.correctionRoute: Verify static meaning first, then restrict interpolation claims to sampled supported cases.

activityDesigns.cumulativeArtifact: A defect clinic repairs four unrelated incidents using first-divergence traces rather than broad fallback additions.
activityDesigns.startingState: Each incident includes misleading appearance evidence and one broken cascade, substitution, cycle, or registration assumption.
activityDesigns.completionState: Each minimal repair passes its original and changed regression case with a causal explanation.
activityDesigns.feedbackContract: Feedback reports observed divergence and evidence surface, not the defect label or patch.
activityDesigns.hintContract: Hints narrow to cascade, token stream, dependency graph, or registration contract after a committed prediction.
activityDesigns.correctionContract: A diagnosis is accepted only after the repair survives a different mutation.

interactions.title: Predict the apparent token defect
interactions.learnerAction: Predict whether a wrong badge value begins in ordinary cascade, custom-property inheritance, or consuming-property defaulting before inspecting tools.
interactions.artifactChange: Clinic record gains a falsifiable first-divergence hypothesis.

evidence.observable: Prediction names one value stage and the evidence that can disprove it.
evidence.changedCase: Reorder the competing layer and move the badge beneath a different owner.

interactions.feedbackTarget: First diagnosis based only on rendered color.
interactions.correctionRoute: Trace declarations and computed custom property on the consumer before editing.

interactions.title: Inspect fallback failure
interactions.learnerAction: Inspect why a var() fallback does not rescue a present token stream that becomes invalid only in its consuming shorthand.
interactions.artifactChange: Clinic record gains the exact substituted shorthand and invalid stage.

evidence.observable: Trace distinguishes available variable from invalid post-substitution consumer grammar.
evidence.changedCase: Make the property missing, then restore it with a token stream valid for only one longhand.

interactions.feedbackTarget: First assumption that fallback catches all later grammar errors.
interactions.correctionRoute: Retokenize var(), substitute it, and parse the complete shorthand separately.

interactions.title: Debug an indirect cycle
interactions.learnerAction: Debug an indirect per-element cycle hidden across three custom properties and repair the ownership error causing it.
interactions.artifactChange: Clinic record gains a minimal cycle repair and graph regression.

evidence.observable: Repair removes only the causal edge and restores intended computed consumers.
evidence.changedCase: Move one declaration to the parent and add the reverse reference inside a fallback.

interactions.feedbackTarget: First deleted dependency unrelated to the actual cycle.
interactions.correctionRoute: Mark all edges and cycle members, then repair the earliest ownership violation.

interactions.title: Test registration repair
interactions.learnerAction: Test and explain a repair for an invalid @property rule, syntax-invalid winner, false @supports claim, and broken interpolation assumption.
interactions.artifactChange: Clinic closes with descriptor, cascade, support, and interpolation regressions.

evidence.observable: Repair is valid, cascade-neutral, support-bounded, and usable without interpolation.
evidence.changedCase: Use a dependent initial value, make a higher-priority value invalid, and remove registration support.

interactions.feedbackTarget: First untested descriptor, precedence, support, or motion claim.
interactions.correctionRoute: Test each contract dimension independently, then rerun the integrated changed case.

interactions.title: Explain the causal repair
interactions.learnerAction: Explain why the minimal clinic repairs work across cascade, inheritance, substitution, cycles, registration, support, and motion evidence.
interactions.artifactChange: Clinic closes with a first-divergence causal account.

evidence.observable: Explanation links each symptom to the earliest broken value stage and bounds the verified repair.
evidence.changedCase: Change precedence, consumer grammar, dependency ownership, registration validity, and motion support.

interactions.feedbackTarget: First repair claim unsupported by a before-and-after trace.
interactions.correctionRoute: Return to the earliest changed evidence stage and revise the causal account only.

activityDesigns.cumulativeArtifact: An independent community dashboard gains an original reusable-value architecture and stakeholder evidence package.
activityDesigns.startingState: Learner receives a new semantic dashboard, behavior requirements, reader constraints, and test environments but no token list or property architecture.
activityDesigns.completionState: Learner builds, mutates, and defends an original system with bounded registration and motion choices.
activityDesigns.feedbackContract: Lab feedback reports failed acceptance evidence only after submission.
activityDesigns.hintContract: On-request hints restate the requirement and evidence category without naming properties or code.
activityDesigns.correctionContract: Failed evidence requires a new mutation and causal revision before acceptance.

interactions.title: Build the provenance baseline
interactions.learnerAction: Build a value-authority ledger for dashboard regions, components, states, and reader overrides before defining custom properties.
interactions.artifactChange: Dashboard gains an independently authored value ownership plan.

evidence.observable: Plan traces every shared decision to an owner and preserves user-origin authority.
evidence.changedCase: Add a region, reparent a component, and introduce a reader important declaration.

interactions.feedbackTarget: First ownership claim without a consumer and changed requirement.
interactions.correctionRoute: Trace the disputed value through all consumers and revise its narrowest owner.

interactions.title: Build reusable value paths
interactions.learnerAction: Build scoped unregistered properties and bounded fallback chains for dashboard policies without abstracting unrelated literals.
interactions.artifactChange: Dashboard consumes its original reusable-value contracts.

evidence.observable: Every consumer parses after substitution and every fallback corresponds to an intentional optional input.
evidence.changedCase: Remove inputs, empty one value, change consumer grammar, and reorder a layer.

interactions.feedbackTarget: First unjustified abstraction, fallback, or invalid consumer.
interactions.correctionRoute: Reconstruct the exact token and consumer path, then make the smallest ownership repair.

interactions.title: Test dependency invariants
interactions.learnerAction: Test dashboard dependency graphs for direct, indirect, fallback-edge, inherited, and locally overridden cases.
interactions.artifactChange: Dashboard gains dependency and invalidity regressions.

evidence.observable: Graphs remain acyclic in normal cases and identify exact invalid nodes in injected failures.
evidence.changedCase: Close three different cycles and move one edge across an inheritance boundary.

interactions.feedbackTarget: First untested edge or incorrect invalidity boundary.
interactions.correctionRoute: Rebuild the per-element graph and add a parallel regression for the repaired edge.

interactions.title: Build selected registrations
interactions.learnerAction: Build only registrations justified by typed validation, inheritance control, or interpolation and document why other properties remain unregistered.
interactions.artifactChange: Dashboard gains a bounded registered-property contract set.

evidence.observable: Every rule is valid, cascade-neutral, support-tested, and tied to a concrete behavior need.
evidence.changedCase: Invalidate descriptors and values, flip inheritance, and remove registration support.

interactions.feedbackTarget: First registration without behavior value or complete contract evidence.
interactions.correctionRoute: Compare registered and unregistered outcomes, then retain only evidence-backed registration.

interactions.title: Test typed motion boundaries
interactions.learnerAction: Test typed interpolation samples, interruption, endpoint meaning, reduced motion, and unsupported registration for dashboard status changes.
interactions.artifactChange: Dashboard gains a motion and support matrix.

evidence.observable: State meaning survives every environment while intermediate claims match sampled typed behavior.
evidence.changedCase: Interrupt at two times, request reduced motion, change type, and disable registration.

interactions.feedbackTarget: First environment where state meaning depends on interpolation.
interactions.correctionRoute: Restore static endpoint meaning, then retest optional motion separately.

interactions.title: Defend the property system
interactions.learnerAction: Defend dashboard naming, ownership, cascade, substitution, dependency, registration, support, and motion decisions to maintenance and accessibility stakeholders.
interactions.artifactChange: Dashboard closes with an evidence-linked architecture decision record.

evidence.observable: Defense distinguishes verified behavior, draft bounds, tradeoffs, rejected abstraction, and unresolved risks.
evidence.changedCase: Add a new component owner, reader style, invalid token, cycle, unsupported engine, and reduced-motion user.

interactions.feedbackTarget: First claim exceeding tested behavior or omitting a stakeholder constraint.
interactions.correctionRoute: Bind the claim to one matrix result and revise unsupported architecture conclusions.

activityDesigns.cumulativeArtifact: A delayed casebook retrieves cascade, inheritance, substitution, dependency, registration, and interpolation from unfamiliar evidence.
activityDesigns.startingState: Learner receives new source, computed values, trees, graphs, and timelines without prior answers.
activityDesigns.completionState: All four concepts are retrieved and misses are corrected on different cases before rescheduling.
activityDesigns.feedbackContract: Feedback names the first missing evidence category after commitment.
activityDesigns.hintContract: On-request hints cue a value stage, then provide a smaller parallel trace.
activityDesigns.correctionContract: Every miss requires explanation and a changed parallel result.

interactions.title: Retrieve cascade and defaulting
interactions.learnerAction: Retrieve winner, inheritance, initial, unset, revert, and computed-value consequences for an unfamiliar component tree.
interactions.artifactChange: Casebook gains an ordinary-value provenance trace.

evidence.observable: Trace applies precedence before property-specific defaulting.
evidence.changedCase: Move the element, toggle importance, and change an inherited property.

interactions.feedbackTarget: First skipped precedence or defaulting decision.
interactions.correctionRoute: Rebuild the tied candidates, then apply defaulting on a new tree.

interactions.title: Predict unfamiliar substitution
interactions.learnerAction: Predict case-sensitive lookup, same-element computed tokens, inheritance, nested fallback, and consumer validity in a new stylesheet.
interactions.artifactChange: Casebook gains a complete unregistered substitution trace.

evidence.observable: Prediction separates variable availability from post-substitution grammar.
evidence.changedCase: Change name case, owner, fallback commas, and consumer property.

interactions.feedbackTarget: First lookup, inheritance, tokenization, or consumer-grammar mistake.
interactions.correctionRoute: Compute the custom property on the consumer, substitute, then validate.

interactions.title: Correct fallback overreach
interactions.learnerAction: Correct a claim that empty, missing, guaranteed-invalid, cycle-invalid, and invalid consumer values all invoke the same fallback.
interactions.artifactChange: Casebook gains a corrected invalidity classification.

evidence.observable: Each class receives its exact var() and consuming-property consequence.
evidence.changedCase: Replace empty with initial, close a cycle, and change consumer grammar.

interactions.feedbackTarget: First invalidity class assigned the wrong recovery.
interactions.correctionRoute: Classify the referenced computed value before evaluating fallback or consumer parsing.

interactions.title: Explain cycle membership
interactions.learnerAction: Explain direct, indirect, fallback-edge, and cross-inheritance dependencies and identify exact invalid nodes per element.
interactions.artifactChange: Casebook gains an unfamiliar dependency graph explanation.

evidence.observable: Explanation includes every edge and invalidates only cycle members on the element.
evidence.changedCase: Move an edge to a parent and add a self-reference inside fallback.

interactions.feedbackTarget: First omitted edge or incorrect per-element boundary.
interactions.correctionRoute: Redraw literal var() edges before identifying connected cycles.

interactions.title: Retrieve registration requirements
interactions.learnerAction: Retrieve syntax, inherits, initial-value, computational independence, computed validation, registration selection, and cascade neutrality requirements.
interactions.artifactChange: Casebook gains a registration validity checklist.

evidence.observable: Checklist distinguishes universal syntax, invalid rules, selected registration, and invalid typed winners.
evidence.changedCase: Omit descriptors, duplicate rules, use em initial value, and invalidate the winner.

interactions.feedbackTarget: First descriptor, selection, or computed-validation misconception.
interactions.correctionRoute: Validate the rule, select registration, cascade declarations, then compute.

interactions.title: Predict typed interpolation
interactions.learnerAction: Predict typed samples, interruption, static endpoints, reduced-motion, false @supports evidence, and unregistered behavior in a new timeline.
interactions.artifactChange: Casebook closes with a bounded interpolation and support matrix.

evidence.observable: Prediction binds intermediate behavior to type and observed support while preserving static meaning.
evidence.changedCase: Change type, interrupt timing, disable registration, and request reduced motion.

interactions.feedbackTarget: First interpolation or support claim beyond evidence.
interactions.correctionRoute: Verify registration and endpoints, then sample only the supported motion case.

activityDesigns.cumulativeArtifact: An independent performance check produces answers, code, debugging, and defense across novel reusable-value cases.
activityDesigns.startingState: Learner receives unfamiliar requirements and fixtures with no prior artifacts or hints.
activityDesigns.completionState: All four concepts pass changed-case server-side evidence or route to targeted retrieval and a different form.
activityDesigns.feedbackContract: Assessment reports evidence categories only after submission and never exposes canonical answers.
activityDesigns.hintContract: No assessment hints or answer reveal are available.
activityDesigns.correctionContract: Each failure routes to prerequisite retrieval and a novel reassessment variant.

interactions.title: Answer value provenance
interactions.learnerAction: Answer the winner, inherited or initial source, and computed result for ordinary and custom declarations in a novel tree.
interactions.artifactChange: Check records an independent provenance answer.

evidence.observable: Answer applies ordinary cascade and defaulting before substitution.
evidence.changedCase: Reparent the consumer, add user importance, and remove a local value.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Cascade and defaulting invariant.
interactions.correctionRoute: Return to provenance retrieval, then answer a new tree.

interactions.title: Code bounded reusable values
interactions.learnerAction: Code purpose-named scoped properties and fallbacks that satisfy independent ownership and consumer-grammar requirements.
interactions.artifactChange: Check implementation gains original reusable-value contracts.

evidence.observable: Every property has justified scope and every consumer remains valid under mutation.
evidence.changedCase: Move components, remove optional values, change name case, and swap consumer grammar.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Ownership, fallback, and substitution invariant.
interactions.correctionRoute: Return to bounded substitution, then code a different fixture.

interactions.title: Classify invalid values
interactions.learnerAction: Classify empty, missing, initial, cycle-invalid, and invalid-consumer cases and answer whether var() fallback runs.
interactions.artifactChange: Check records an invalidity and recovery table.

evidence.observable: Every class receives its exact computed and consumer result.
evidence.changedCase: Convert each case into another invalidity class and alter property inheritance.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Invalidity classification invariant.
interactions.correctionRoute: Return to substitution stages, then classify a new table.

interactions.title: Debug dependency invalidity
interactions.learnerAction: Debug a multi-property cycle, identify exact invalid nodes per element, and implement the smallest ownership repair.
interactions.artifactChange: Check records a repaired graph and regression.

evidence.observable: Repair removes the causal edge and preserves unaffected dependencies.
evidence.changedCase: Move an edge across inheritance and close another cycle through fallback.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Dependency graph and minimal repair invariant.
interactions.correctionRoute: Return to graph tracing, then debug a new dependency set.

interactions.title: Code a valid registration
interactions.learnerAction: Code a justified @property contract and prove descriptor validity, computational independence, inheritance, and typed invalid behavior.
interactions.artifactChange: Check implementation gains a verified registration.

evidence.observable: Rule is valid and computed results match the registered contract.
evidence.changedCase: Remove descriptors, use dependent initial value, flip inherits, and submit the wrong type.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Registration descriptor and computed behavior invariant.
interactions.correctionRoute: Return to contract validation, then register a different type.

interactions.title: Debug registered precedence
interactions.learnerAction: Debug a trace that revives a valid lower-priority declaration after a typed-invalid registered winner.
interactions.artifactChange: Check records corrected cascade-then-validation reasoning.

evidence.observable: Ordinary cascade winner remains fixed before registered computed validation.
evidence.changedCase: Reorder layers, toggle importance, and invalidate a different winner.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Cascade neutrality invariant.
interactions.correctionRoute: Return to cascade precedence, then debug a new registered conflict.

interactions.title: Code bounded typed motion
interactions.learnerAction: Code typed interpolation with meaningful static endpoints, reduced-motion behavior, interruption tests, and observed support evidence.
interactions.artifactChange: Check gains a typed timeline and no-motion result.

evidence.observable: Samples match the registered type and meaning survives without interpolation.
evidence.changedCase: Interrupt motion, disable registration, request reduced motion, and change type.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Typed interpolation and static meaning invariant.
interactions.correctionRoute: Return to endpoints and type behavior, then code a new timeline.

interactions.title: Defend reusable-value architecture
interactions.learnerAction: Defend property ownership, abstraction limits, cascade, substitution, invalidity, registration, support, and motion for a novel interface.
interactions.artifactChange: Check closes with an evidence-bounded architecture defense.

evidence.observable: Defense ties each choice to changed behavior and states draft, support, and accessibility limits.
evidence.changedCase: Add new consumers, reader styles, invalid inputs, cycles, unsupported registration, and reduced motion.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Integrated transfer and evidence invariant.
interactions.correctionRoute: Return to the first failed concept gate, then defend a different architecture.

authorshipBoundary.interpretation: This file fixes evidence, sequence, interaction, feedback, and correction requirements only; original learner-facing explanations, fixtures, checks, answers, and responsive workspace behavior remain unauthored and unpublished.

gaps: Independent subject, instructional-design, assessment, accessibility, safety, and duplication reviews have not accepted this step architecture.
Learner-facing copy, source fixtures, canonical server-side checks, persisted correction scheduling, cross-browser support evidence, and learner pilots do not yet exist.
